reviews.review_text:
Dead Island 2 is simple: you just run around a great looking LA and smash zombies. The combat feels heavy, the weapon modding is fun, and the gore mechanics are incredibly detailed. It doesn't take itself seriously and it runs smoothly.

If you want a straightforward game to turn your brain off and break some, it’s worth playing.

If you're looking for an immersive zombie apocalypse with moral choices and all that, you're looking at the wrong game.

If you want pure zombie slaying action with insanely fun ways, where you have a huge selection of improvised weapons, environmental kills and customizable skills, then you're in the right place.

Do you want to feel like the main character of an absurd b-movie, slashing your way through zombies with heads rolling and eyeballs flying everywhere? As if you're in a Tarantino movie or something like Kung Fury?? 

I know you said "Hell yeah!" Well... this game delivers exactly that.

Dead Island 2 -review
graphics- really good
gameplay- very good, I really enjoyed it
audience- for older teens or adults
PC requirements- decent my 6 years old PC ran it well
game size- little bit big 70 GB
difficulty- easy to learn the combat
story- good story lame ending
price- 50 eur- buy the game when it on sale for like 8 eur
bugs- some achievements were buggy as hell
game time- full achievement + 2 playthroughs -70 hours for me just right
?/10- 9/10 great zombie game, I really enjoyed it. I can only recommend !!

Dead Island 2 is a giant, dumb, messy bag of laughs. While Dead Island’s setting was an actual island, Dead Island 2’s setting is…well…..Los Angeles. So no longer an island? Nevertheless, the experience is the same glorious cocktail of guns, melee, and complete zombie dismemberment. 
Some of the Steam reviews mention the game lacking depth and the combat monotonous. The game is called Dead Island 2! It is about zombies! The narrative was not concocted  by Walt Whitman. It was written by nerds. Dead Island 2 is exactly what it advertises; loud, bloody, chaotic, and set in a city that was arguably already a zombie apocalypse.

PROS
*The setting is gorgeous and the attention to detail amazing. Running through the mansions of the Los Angeles ultra-wealthy while those same ultra-wealthy try to eat your face? Love it. 
*Surprisingly, the characters likable and funny. Well, all of them but one. She is probably too close to true humanity for my liking. I played as Ryan and felt pretty naive when I realized half way through the game he was NOT an actual firefighter, but a stripper. Either way, he was a fun character. 
*The semi-open world is carved into distinct areas, and somehow the developers made it feel much larger than it actually is. The player is rewarded for exploring all the nooks and crannies. 
*Haus DLC. The premise is basically a short story written by Clive Barker. Ultra rich looking for immortality at a health spa that results in pig masks, rubber suits, hooked chains, and bottomless pits. This was the closest this game came to unsettling and it delivers. 

CONS
*Combat. Yes, it can be monotonous. That is what the crafting tables are for. Slap some fire, acid, or electricity on your pool noodle and suddenly everything feels fresh again. The game gives you the tools; use them.
*SoHo DLC. Wow. This was a slog fest. The setting of a music festival was terrific, but where it falls flat is the story and addition of a certain enemy type. The story was already a drag , but let us add transporting blob monsters so annoying that you will want to eat glass. I found myself running away from everything just to enjoy the  video game cardio. 
*Influencer Amanda Styles. Holy hell, the voice and vernacular of this character. I have never wanted to beat a NPC to death with a stop sign more than this character.

Honestly I don't get the hate for this game, I thought it was awesome, fun gameplay, unique enemies, some of the best gore I've ever seen in a  videogame, sure the gunplay is limited, but who cares? This game deserves far more respect than its given

Awesome environmental design and storytelling, disgustingly gory combat, and entertaining quips from the characters culminate in a nice old-school zombie killing powertrip. Story is nothing to write home about, though the NPCs certainly have personality. The game's simplicity is its most divisive characteristic, but I found myself appreciating it more and more as the game continued.

Dead Island 2 is a linear, level gated, zombie slaying sandbox game with terrible story and meaningless dialogues. Characters constantly make bad jokes and remarks as you play, like they are in some bad sitcom. The only redeeming parts are sandbox, co-op, melee combat, weapon mods, abilities and overall visuals. Besides that it doesn't have any memorable characters apart from Sam B.

Story - It's bad, and it get worse the further you get. DLC's expand the bad writing even more.

Interactions - Tasteless dialogues in 80% of the time like it's a Saints Row game or something.

Sandbox - When you unlock multiple areas you are generally free to move between them, explore, scavenge, slay, collect collectibles, etc. It's constantly refreshes loot so you can game endlessly.

Co-Op - It's great, on the most part. It's way more fun to bash and slice everything around with buds. Can be a bit laggy if you are far from Host, but overall great experience if your main focus is just a brainless zombie slaughter.

Combat - Great variety of melee weapons you can upgrade, from blunt to cutting to piercing to smashing. Good damage model on zombies. There are firearms you can also upgrade, some better than others, craft ammo, etc.

Mods - Various elemental mods to put on weapons. Electric, fire, acid, force, bleed, etc. useful to have at leas one of each since game tend to throw different elemental zombies at you, that can be immune to one or the other. You can also put mods made from zombie parts with different effects, like restore stamina or health, give damage buff, add elemental explosion on kill, etc., many variations of those.

Abilities - You have fury similar to older games but now with more variety and options to customize how you want to play a specific character. Each character have stats, strengths and weaknesses. Some have high health, high critical chance on attacks, more toughness, more stamina or agility, etc. You equip active and passive abilities using cards, giving you special ways to survive and slay zombies. It's pretty good.

Visuals - Game looks stunning. Has day and night setting. Texture quality on surfaces, weapons, characters and zombies is good. Some environments can be a bit too sterile, houses too clean, like it's not even zombie apocalypse. Overall model, prop quality is good.

Technical - Performance is ideal, at leas for me it was. No errors, no crashes, no overheating, no save issues. Only had a minor lag with Host, but nothing game breaking. Controls are smooth, animations fluid.

Consensus - Bought DI2 on launch on Epic, was enjoyable experience. Story wasn't that great but everything else scaled out positive. Bought this on Steam recently, as it was on discount for 5 bucks, well worth it if you turn off brain from the terrible writing and just enjoy slaying zombies with friends.

An incredibly fun zombie melee experience, and a complete steal when it goes on sale. I highly recommend the Ultimate Edition as those DLCs pack enough gameplay length that the extra goes a long way.

I really enjoyed nearly every weapon variation, and that says a lot. Every hit has a glorious impact, and I never grew tired of just running around killing zombies.

The story is breezy and never takes itself seriously. The side quests are quirky and fun, never outstaying their welcome. The combat has a fantastic escalation that grows with depth and enemy variety.

I don't know how they did it, but they did. This is the best zombie melee game out there right now, and Dead Island 3 can't get here soon enough.
So far the game isn't totally bad, enjoying it at the moment, about 40% through the story and still compelled to play through it. Not bad.

This game is just.... fantastic, the visuals. the design of the and attention to detail is amazing, its one of those zombie games that stick to the theme and make it zombie versus human. The combat gameplay is solid, the gore on this game is one of the most intense I have ever seen. The stories are not to bad, some of the characters you select when you are playing, sound kind of cheesy and kind of lame, but overall the game  this game is very very good. One of my favorite zombie games of all time,  Wish the game was a bit bigger and had a few more stories/ side quests, I loved exploring LA and going into these beautiful houses, wish there were a few of those but overall still very much enjoyed this game a solid 9.5/10

I HAD NO RIGHT to like this game so much!

My entire gaming experience should've boiled down to not enjoying this type of game for more than a couple of hours. But alas, it hooked me on beating the entire campaign while simultaneously covering half of the side quests.

My initial anticipation was to traverse infected LA, and I also knew it was going to be quite a silly experience. And boy did it bring me joy - the gore was goring, the immaculate graphics were shining, npcs were joyfully goofy and it was giving off some gta vibes.

Someone out there called it a "popcorn game", and so let it be a perfect example of it.

Dead Island 2 is one of the best zombie games out there.

Features fun and satisfying combat mechanics and crafting, an awesome soundtrack and sound design and a well-done story that feels like the prequel to Dead Island 3, just because it ended suddenly in what I thought was the middle of it, but it makes sense now. I guess time flies when you’re having fun.

The graphics were really great, proving you don't need sh*tty RTX or Lumen garbage to create beautifully detailed scenes and lighting. The attention to detail in level design and textures really popped and looked better than most AAA games out there.

I've never been to LA but the level design was so well done I kept seeing a bunch o comments of how realistic it is compared to the real LA, or how great they capture LA's essence. So, after watching a comparison video of the locations in game vs the real LA, I was stunned. They did an awesome job! I feel like I know some parts of LA now, it’s crazy.

Awesome fun game, recommended to all gamers.
10/10

Can't wait for the next one!

Well, Dead Island 2 is a tricky game. It went through development hell, changing hands between multiple studios. So, did Dambuster Studios manage to make a good sequel?

I was always pretty skeptical about the first game. Its narrative felt muddled and, frankly, boring. Naturally, I was just as skeptical about the sequel, especially after all the studio changes. But my doubts were completely unwarranted - completely. The end result is a very solid game, one that’s absolutely competitive within its genre - and with Dying Light in particular.

In fact, the two franchises are locked in a quiet rivalry, to the point of near-imitation. Dead Island 2 even recycles an almost identical sewer sequence that introduces the exploding zombie - a moment lifted practically beat for beat from Dying Light. It’s the kind of direct nod that shows just how much these series keep an eye on each other.

Level Design
It’s worth starting with the first thing that catches your eye: the visuals. The graphics, the saturated colours, the wonderfully unforced atmosphere. At first glance, Dead Island 2’s environments seem a bit lacklustre, but as you play you get used to them and even start overlooking certain things. The small budget is visible to the naked eye, yet so is the tremendous amount of work put into the world. And once you settle in, you begin to notice all the little details the developers crafted. For example, a huge work on the environment, after the end of the game you notice how many animations have been prepared for npc in the world, the world of the game even looks very lively as for zombie-action.

Characters
You can choose from six different characters, each with their own backstory, perks and stats - very much in the style of the first game or Left 4 Dead. Progression here takes the form of ability cards that you can swap out on the fly.

Gameplay
The combat and movement aren’t radically different from the first game or Dying Light - except there’s no grappling hook or paraglider; here you’re on foot. When it comes to attention to detail, the work done on the zombie bodies is impossible to overlook. You simply won’t find a more detailed, gory mess in any other zombie game. There are also interactions with liquids, you can throw just about anything, and the heavy emphasis here is squarely on the gameplay.

Storyline
The story starts off painfully cliché, but as it goes on it picks up so much momentum that by the end you’re genuinely on the edge of your seat waiting for the payoff. The plot is actually really good - you just have to push through what is essentially a tutorial-heavy first half. Same the main character is constantly talking, cracking jokes and whatnot, and it makes the experience feel very alive.

DLC
The add-ons offer a bunch of weapons and character skins as you’d expect from similar games, but there are also two story-driven DLCs.
Haus
If the main game wasn’t brutal enough for you, there’s a wonderfull world waiting. It’s a place where the zombie-slaying action morphs into psychological horror - laced with avantgarde and psychedelic notes. Think severed talking heads, bathtubs brimming with blood, latex-suited zombies leaping out of old pot-bellied TVs, initiation rituals, and etc.
Sola
A clear example of why Woodstock was stopped - so as not to start an epidemic of zombie virus.

Conclusion 
Dead Island 2 isn’t a perfect game, but it is a very good one. You could even call it a reference-quality sequel - it takes everything from the first game and pushes it to its absolute limit, Also, I can't help but note some obvious downsides. For starters, the music—or rather, the lack of it. How does a game all about hacking up zombies end up with such a boring soundtrack? I'm not saying it needs the raw, adrenaline-fueled energy of Payday 2 or Doom Eternal, but come on. There are a few decent tracks scattered around, but that's about it. I'd also love to see a proper difficulty system instead of just a handful of ability cards with over-the-top "mythical" descriptions that barely change the gameplay. But anyway resulting in one of the best zombie games of our time.
__________________________________

Что ж, Dead Island 2 - игра непростая. Она прошла через производственный ад, сменив за время разработки не одну и не две студии. Так удалось ли Dambuster Studios сделать достойный сиквел?

Я всегда относился к первой части весьма скептически. Её повествование казалось путаным и, честно говоря, скучным. Разумеется, с тем же скепсисом я ждал и продолжения, особенно после всех этих смен студий. Но мои сомнения оказались совершенно напрасными - абсолютно напрасными. Итогом стала очень крепкая игра, абсолютно конкурентоспособная в своём жанре - и в особенности с Dying Light.

По сути, две франшизы находятся в тихом соперничестве, доходящем почти до прямого подражания. В Dead Island 2 даже воспроизводится практически идентичная сцена в канализации, знакомящая игрока с взрывающимся зомби - момент, почти идентичен аналогичному из Dying Light. Такой прямой кивок ясно показывает, насколько пристально эти серии следят друг за другом.

Дизайн уровней
Начать стоит с того, что бросается в глаза в первую очередь: с визуала. Графика, насыщенные цвета, замечательно ненавязчивая атмосфера. Поначалу окружение Dead Island 2 может показаться бедноватым, но по мере игры привыкаешь и даже перестаёшь замечать некоторые огрехи. Скромный бюджет виден невооружённым глазом, однако столь же заметен и колоссальный объём проделанной работы. А когда осваиваешься, начинаешь подмечать массу мелких деталей, над которыми трудились разработчики. Например огромная работа над окружением, уже после конца игры замечаешь сколько анимаций заготовили для нпс в мире, мир игры даже выглядит очень живым как для зомби экшена.

Персонажи
На выбор дают шестерых разных героев, у каждого свои предыстория, перки и характеристики - в духе первой части или Left 4 Dead. Прогрессия здесь реализована в виде карт способностей, которые можно менять прямо на ходу.

Геймплей
Боевая система и перемещение не слишком отличаются от первой части или Dying Light - разве что тут нет крюка-кошки и параплана, передвигаться придётся пешком. Что касается внимания к деталям, невозможно не отметить работу над телами зомби. Такой проработанной, зубодробительной расчленёнки вы не найдёте ни в одной другой зомби-игре. Есть и взаимодействие с жидкостями, кидать можно практически всё что угодно, а главный акцент здесь сделан именно на геймплей.

Сюжет
Хоть по началу история и кажется да боли клишированной, но ближе к концу набирает такие обороты, что ты в предвкушении ждешь развязку. Сюжет здесь классный, но придётся вытерпеть по сути «обучающую» первую половину. Так же главный герой постоянно болтает, шутит и всё в таком духе, благодаря чему происходящее ощущается очень живым.

DLC
Дополнения представлены ворохом оружия и обликов для персонажей, как и во всех подобных играх, но есть и два сюжетных дополнения.
Haus
Если жестокости основной игры вам показалось мало, добро пожаловать в дивный мир. Здесь истребление зомби превращается в психологический хоррор с налётом авангарда и психоделики - говорящие отрубленные головы, наполненные кровью ванны, зомби в латексных костюмах, выпрыгивающие из старых пузатых телевизоров, ритуалы посвящения и многое другое.
Sola
Наглядный пример почему Вудсток перестали проводить - что бы не начать эпидемию зомби вируса.

Полный перевод заключения не уместился в обзор.
Dead Island 2 не идеальная игра, но очень хорошая. Её можно даже назвать эталонным сиквелом - она берёт всё из первой части и доводит до абсолюта в результате получается один из лучших зомби-экшенов нашего времени.

Difficulty balancing is a nightmare. Under-leveled by a little and everything becomes a literal tank made to eat weapon durability, at-level and basic zombies become fodder while elites are still a nightmare, over-leveled even a little and boss enemies fall over with 1 combo.

Not even gonna go into detail with the bugs, just know I lost count of how many times zombies suddenly materialized in front of me.

Good game that went through development hell and came out as a winner. I like damage you can do to zombies. Story is meh, but killing zombies is fun. I still prefer Dying Light series a lot more, but this is not a bad game at all

My favorite game of the decade so far! Originally got it free on Epic, played all the way through and loved it so much I re-bought it on Steam! Would recommend full price, but it goes on sale couple times a year, usually around $10 bucks and a steal at that.
Unique in games nowadays, in that it keeps most what made the original games fun and adds modern features. I really liked the original games all the way back in the 360 era, so this one is the sequel I was hoping for. With the exception of scripted story parts, you can still play the way you want. That includes hoping on a raised surface and loping heads. Or you can still throw melee weapons, my go-to. Play at your own pace, no rush, just chill gameplay.
Basically its an action zombie looter shooter, with minor survival and rpg elements. Its level based, but they're pretty big and feel almost open world. Story is standard zombie outbreak fare, but good enough. Would make a fun action flick. Plays well on older hardware.

This the type of ♥♥♥♥ you put on after a hard day's work in middle school and procrastinate doing homework with your buddies for hours. Kids are gonna have hell-la nostalgia for this game in ten years.

It's like the most 6/10 experience ever. But killing zombies stays fun and the dismemberment/gore system looks REALLY good. The story abruptly ends just when it feels like it's going somewhere, and the dialogue is unapologetically cheesy. It's a great time with friends, but dear GOD, do NOT buy this game on full price wait till it comes around on one of those JUICY 90% sales.

Honestly the game was decently enjoyable especially for a couple bucks on sale, especially if you plan to play it with a friend. If you peruse the rest of the steam reviews, you'll see lots of players corroborating the praise for the satisfying zombie destruction physics and combat, visuals, and performance. It's really quite a solid game from a technical perspective. Plenty of people seemed to complain about the writing feeling like a Netflix original gen-z cringe fest, but honestly I felt like it came off as ironic and self-aware, rather than something like Borderlands 3 which seems to unironically think that type of humour is funny. There's a shocking amount of incidental dialogue between NPCs that you aren't forced to sit through but it can be entertaining to listen to, and it adds realism to the characters instead of feeling like they're quest billboards dressed up in human skin. I enjoyed both the gameplay and characters a fair bit more than I was expecting to, which is definitely a welcome surprise.

There are a few annoying things about the game as a whole but honestly they're minor nitpicks that don't distract too much from the core experience, with one pretty glaring exception-- 

The ending flat out isn't an ending. I won't spoil it, but it sort of feels like things really gets going, you do one mission which feels like the climax of the second act of the story, which would then lead into a short-but-sweet final act, where the remaining major plot threads get tied up--only that doesn't happen. The game cuts to your character fighting some zombies in a cutscene, then just rolls credits. Maybe this would have been acceptable if they had a DLC planned for this portion of the story, but from what I can tell, both story DLCs for this game take place during the main story rather than after it, and are sort of just side stories which maybe offer some context to the main game but don't continue it. It's a shame, because I was actually somewhat enjoying the characters by the end of the story, and I was interested in seeing where it went. It doesn't even feel like sequel bait, they just genuinely didn't make the end of the game and called it a day. 

Other than that though, it's a pretty good game.

This is pretty fun. I like the idea of implementing curveballs, removing the need to worry about hoarding throwables and focusing on the game's main point: killing zombies.

My only complaint is that they took away the analog dual stick combat option from the first game, which allowed you to chose the direction of the blows. That is what made the first game stand out from most melee combat games.

Also, I played it right after Dying Light 2 and the story is too similar. Good thing DI2 doesn't take itself too seriously.

Unfortunately this is a hard one. If you don't pay attention to the absolute garbage of story and cringiest characters ever, it's a pretty fun zombie game. Also it automatically installs epic garbage on your system, for that alone this deserves a negative review. Grab only on a sale.

Am I happy to finish Dead Island 2, even if I believed for almost a decade of silence we would never see this? Yes. Is it a good game? Unfortunately, no.

Although the game features so many weapons and variants with elemental upgrades, there is no real strategy behind them. I didn't feel a big difference whether I put a zombie on fire, poisoned it, or caused a bleeding status. Even electricity, which shocks other zombies nearby, had too little of an impact to allow for true tactics. This resulted in a combat system so boring that it motivated me to search for better weapons and upgrades—not for fun, but simply to skip these repetitive combat encounters as fast as possible.

And if you think there is at least a clever way to attack weak spots on the zombies, I have to disappoint you there as well. It is additionally frustrating because the developers put so much work into the visual damage system, which ultimately has zero impact from a gameplay perspective. For example, if you injure the hell out of a big zombie's leg until only the bone structure is visible, the leg will still receive the exact same damage as it did in the beginning. The bone only breaks once the zombie hits 0 HP.

The world itself, with its various locations, was actually fun to explore, but purely to see the environments. Do not mistake this exploration for a treasure hunt to find rare weapons or resources. It suffers from the same problem as the combat: there is no actual gameplay value in scavenging every building. This becomes especially noticeable when you realize that the open areas have a day and night version—and, surprise, surprise, they make absolutely no difference to the gameplay. The exact same zombies with the exact same behavior are standing on the exact same spots behind the exact same loot. Therefore, exploring during the night is unnecessary, unless you simply want to see less for some reason. The night doesn't even change dynamically over time; you have to manually select it when traveling to an area.

Despite all these negative aspects, I must admit that the story here was actually good. After playing and seeing so many zombie games with a dark, horror-heavy atmosphere, this one feels like a breath of fresh air, even if it isn't doing anything completely new. Dead Island 2 focuses more on being a fun slasher experience, much like some famous zombie movies. The humor and the overall cleaner aesthetic gave me the opportunity to just chill and even laugh at certain situations—I never knew I could enjoy this type of style in a zombie game.

I also want to respect how well-optimized the technical state is for a game that went through production hell. The graphics look absolutely fantastic, and overall, the game runs incredibly stable, with neither noticeable bugs nor unstable performance.

But at the end of the day, Dead Island 2 is a below-average zombie experience. Sure, the visible damage I can inflict on those zombies is gruesomely good, but I am not a sadist who can be satisfied by that alone. And even if I were, there are other games that also feature great damage systems but have way more to offer. I might recommend this to players who are open to a gameplay system that doesn't need to be tactical in any way, or to those who miss the days when zombie scenarios were pure fun and entertainment. For me personally, this game was a disappointment. Still, it feels incredibly surreal to finally see Dead Island 2 as a published game.

So this game was Epic exclusive for 2 years and cost 70€, I bought it for 7€ recently but Im not even sure its worth that.

Its unpolished. combat sucks and the devs knew it which is why there is a health item every 5cm and a checkpoint before every fight so there is no consequence to dying, light attacks dont stagger, zombies will still attack back, they have more range than the  player which doesnt matter in  1vs1 but they also dont have collision so they can stand inside of each other and attack you alternating which will always hit you. All weapons deal the exact same damage per level there is no variety, when a zombie is  immune to an element the weapon stops doing damage entirely, the game doesnt care that the weapon is a sword that should cut something, if you put a electricity mod on it and your enemy is immune to electricity you will deal 0 damage to that enemy.

Also every zomby is incredible sprongy and it takes between 5 and 50 hits to kill one, putting a claw in their head doesnt kill them youre just fighting HP points no real mechanics for head destruction, you can cut of limbs but thats just for show they will just hit you with their head instead of arms. Boring waste of time.

Also killing a Zombie gives 20 exp and finishing a quest gives 1500 exp or more so fighting isnt rewarding. My first 10 level ups all unlocked cards that only  did something when I jump kick, slide or counter attack, none of  these is exciting or makes me want to level up more, none of these is effective or does enough damage to a zombie to be anything else but a waste of time. 

Also I thought I disable damage numbers and health bars to get more  of a dying  light feeling and make  it more immersive, but then I noticed zombies that will always  oneshot me, yeah you need to see their hp bars so you see when there is a skull but  then all levels are  basically tunnel and the game doesnt want you  to explore  before you finish the main mission so even the free exploration becomes a tunnel of sort, a chore, a checklist to work off after you  worked off the main story line.

Total disappointment on all levels, even the collecables arent voiced its just text, it feels like  the devs knew they didnt  have a good game which is why they made it Epic exclusive so people coundnt write reviews, and every desicion in the game feels like it was dont to stretch gameplay, make fights unusually long, have items break fast and often so people go back to repair, make leveling outside of quests super slow to impossible, make them read every text..... now that I write that down I wanna see what speedrunners have for a time for this game. I say without glitches 1 hour max.
3/10 Game, final music is very good but story is a complete joke, combat is fine but gets boring, gore and the body physics is very nice but this game doesn't deserve a 60$ price tag.
fun game, easy to learn, good variety of weapon, beautiful graphics, has a nice coop crossplay campaing so you can have lots of fun playing with your friends killing zombies together

Dead Island 2 is a decent bit of zombie fun. The combat is satisfying, the gore system is ridiculously detailed, and smashing your way through hordes of the undead never really gets old.

That said, I nearly didn't get to play it at all. I had to completely uninstall Epic Games because the launcher refused to get past its loading screen. Whatever is going on with Epic's launcher and background services, it's an absolute mess and caused more problems than the zombies did.

As for the game itself, my biggest disappointment is how limited it feels. The environments look great, but you're constantly reminded that this isn't an open world experience. Progression is very controlled, areas are segmented, and there's not much freedom to wander off and make your own fun.

Still, despite those issues, it's a solid boredom buster. Jump in, crack some zombie skulls, enjoy the carnage, and switch your brain off for a few hours.

I just can't help feeling that a true open world Dead Island 2 would have been something special. Hopefully that's a direction the series explores in the future.

Overall: Fun, gory, entertaining, but a little too restrictive for its own good.

This game does not deserve the hate that it gets in reviews. It is "just another" zombie shooter, yes; but a good one. The art is good, the level design works well, the voice acting is top notch. The background story is all over the place, but it doesn't get in the way.

There are bugs in the co-op networking. Match-making was consistently unreliable. Workarounds were required to find friends online. Once a game session was set up, and folks had joined, it was solid. However, we had evenings where we couldn't make that initial connection.

There are bugs in the Haus DLC. Towards the end, I fell into a pit and died. The game respawned me ... at the bottom of the same pit. No way out. My wife carried on without me and almost finished the level ... until another bug prevented her from picking up a quest item necessary to progress. Fortunately, we were able to drop the co-op session, restart the mission with my wife as host, and get it done. Haus is the most interesting and inventive content in the game, and so it still gets high marks from me.

Mission instructions were often obscure (even obtuse). Mission guidance in the DLC simply ... stopped after a time: map and compass markers disappeared. I had to alt-tab out to google to find mission details often. This part of the content was a let down: the missions were interesting and the voice acting was excellent, but the nuts-and-bolts part of directing players to objectives was often quite lacking.

Overall, though: a fine co-op zombie shooter.

absolutely awesome game, with fun mechanics, high quality models, and should i say the amount of gore. the story was bland but fun to get through, especially with the final boss fight. the modifications that you can make with your weapons are awesome, with stuff like fire, acid, electrical, and a heavyyy variety of weapons that make the game all the more fun to play.

Amazing Game, Amazing Graphics, Amazing story, Amazing Characters....Did I mention i think this game is amazing? 5\5 stars!!!

Dead Island (2011) was such a letdown for me. I remember being so annoyed by the overall game mechanics, the controls, game-breaking bugs, and the lackluster story. This game improves on all of these aspects and transcends the standard first-person zombie-killing fun. This is my second time playing it—my first playthrough was on PS5, and my second is with my friends on Steam. I'm also mesmerized by the graphics and the color of everything, from the fire that hangs on the Hollywood sign down to the jaw-dropping gore effects. Punching your fist entirely through a zombie’s skull and watching the procedural damage deform in real-time is easily one of the most satisfying loops in a modern action game. Everything is just so detailed and well thought out.
this is a good ass game, the story is like a bad zombie movie that knows its bad but has a huge budget. I love this ♥♥♥♥.
beautiful game, true to form from the original Dead Island, I truly hope they are able to bring out a dead island 3!

Dead Island 2 knows exactly what it is.

More importantly, it knows exactly what it isn't.

It isn't trying to reinvent the zombie genre. It isn't trying to deliver a profound meditation on humanity. It isn't trying to convince me that every zombie game needs parkour, base-building, extraction mechanics, survival systems, crafting trees, settlement management, or seventeen different currencies.

Dead Island 2 looks at all of that and politely says:

"That's nice. Anyway, here's a zombie. Hit it with a wrench."

As someone who loved the original Dead Island, I was honestly just hoping for more of the same. After spending 134 hours with it, I got exactly what I wanted.

The combat is absurdly satisfying.

Some people will call it repetitive.

Those people are technically correct.

I also don't care.

Every bone-crunch, limb sever, skull stab, and perfectly-timed counterattack feels fantastic. I eventually settled into a knife-focused playstyle. Dodge. Counter. Quick stab to the skull. Repeat until the problem stops moving.

And when subtlety failed, I'd shoot the legs out from under a zombie and casually finish the job with a stomp to the head.

Therapy is expensive.

Dead Island 2 was on sale.

The star of the show is easily the FLESH system. It would have been easy for the developers to settle for generic zombie dismemberment and call it a day. Instead, somebody on that team clearly became obsessed with answering increasingly specific questions about zombie anatomy.

"What happens if I slash here?"

"What if I hit it again?"

"What if I electrify it first?"

"What if I really commit to the bit?"

The result is one of the most visually satisfying zombie combat systems I've ever seen. It gives Dead Island 2 its own identity in a genre crowded with competitors.

The story exists.

I don't mean that as an insult.

It exists. It functions. It gets you from one zombie-filled location to another. That's all I really needed it to do.

What surprised me more was how much I enjoyed the world itself. The Los Angeles setting has a great vibe, and the game demonstrates that restraint in world-building can go a long way. It feels lived in without drowning the player in exposition.

There are memorable moments everywhere. My first encounter with an Electric Screamer was pure "What in the fresh hell is THAT?" energy. The tougher enemies introduced in New Game+ add challenge without becoming annoying. And I'll never forget watching a Crusher grab a guy's severed head through a boarded-up window. It was so unexpected that I actually cheered.

My biggest criticism is also one of the game's strengths.

I don't have to turn my brain on.

Sometimes that's wonderful.

Sometimes the grind starts to show.

But honestly? Most of the time I was perfectly happy to switch off the analytical part of my brain and enjoy the wonderfully disgusting ballet of zombie destruction unfolding in front of me.

Compared to games like Left 4 Dead, World War Z, or Dead Rising, Dead Island 2 feels more intimate and visceral. It isn't trying to overwhelm you with scale. It's focused on making every individual zombie encounter feel satisfying.

And it succeeds.

Dead Island 2 understands that most zombie fans already have their favorite zombie game.

It's perfectly content being your second favorite.

The funny thing is that after 134 hours, it might have quietly become one of mine.

Audio keeps cutting out, tried googling it, tried fixing it with what was suggested, didn't fix the issue. why the ♥♥♥♥ is this still a problem when the game was released like 3 years ago? Doesn't get a positive review unless my issue is fixed.

It is best game and DLCS is fun too everything in Dead island 2 is fun

this game is lowkey a masterpiece tbh. they somehow found a way to take the plotline from the first game and make it SO much better and more engaging. the lore is so much more interesting and intense than i anticipated, and the attention to detail is incredible, without retconning ANYTHING from the first game. it feels like a true successor to even with all the development drama and changing of hands. it's kind of a miracle this game was finished at all, let alone so well. the visual storytelling alone is so rich- every single location is entirely unique and filled with character. the loose papers and whiteboards and such all have real lore in them. this game feels like a labor of love, in every way. the story is actually super deep if you really put all the pieces together, and the combat is smooth and SO much fun. paying attention to the little things pays off. i love playing solo and multiplayer, and each character brings something completely different to the gameplay. i'm a newer gamer but strategizing ways to use all of the tools they provide in my solo play is a huge part of the fun for me. it's so satisfying to get through a section alone that i previously went through with support. personally, i find Neighborhood Watch to be the least compelling gameplay, but it's still quite enjoyable. i can't wait to see what they do with more time and resources for Dead Island 3.

((69 hours, nice))

I've never left a review on a Steam game (though I've played for almost 8 years at this point) yet I couldn't resist with this one! 1000/10 PLEASE buy it guys, so worth it!

If you are fine with millennial-older gen z humor and are into B-movie horror comedy where you poke fun at LA culture — celebrity worship, influencers, wellness grifters, and tech bros — this is the game for you. The gore system is disgustingly impressive, and it never takes itself too seriously. Just don't expect the story to wrap up neatly.

Played through the full campaign + DLC, entirely Co-Op. Bought it on sale 2 years after release and have to say the game looks amazing and runs like butter. Fully enjoyed the zombie killing experience with my modded out melee and  ranged weapons. Game can be repetitive but the unique dialogue and individuals you come across the way make it fun. The world of LA is really well set up and everything is well placed. Found ourselves taking time to enjoy the details this game really has. The level of different zombie types is extensive, all with their own individual weaknesses and best ways of attack when going up against them. Zombipedia is pretty cool to keep track of as you progress. If I had one tip it would be to make sure you buy any blueprint or curveball from every vendor you come across to avoid back-tracking missing items/collectables.

The story was not good, I believe the gameplay only gotten fun after you beat game and have weapons you kept for new game+ they made the game only really playable after you beat the main story and get name weapons and or legendary their isn't much too it after anyways. I mean game can be fun depends on how open you are about it. Co-op was just weird to me since they was moving at like 20 fps. I also hated the fact the zombies respawn after barley moving into another location. Now I only play one character but maybe the others could feel different but I feel once you get the skill cards and everything the all probably feel the same and won't really notice a different between them and making them unique other then their hilarious bad voice lines. But I say yes getting on sale is just the a smart thing to do because you will probably enjoy the experience way more knowing you got the game for like $3

While you can still see the skeleton of the franchise that came before it, Dead Island 2 goes out of its way to create its own identity and offers an interesting card mechanic to allow the player to customise their fighting kit however they see fit; and while weapons still break and are rendered useless quite quickly, nothing feels quite like punching and ducking hordes like a boxer on Adderall. Environmental hazards are usually something i avoid in zombie games as it usually ends up being more annoying than helpful, but I found myself chucking car batteries at zombies more often than not or drop kicking them into pits.

I played the main game and DLC in single player so can’t speak to multiplayer experience, but if the opportunity presented itself, I would be completely open to playing it again.
Definitely a game I'll return to in future, and will be keeping my eye open for any announcements from Deep Silver this year.

Excellent game, I liked it much better than Dying Light: The Beast. Lots of similar mechanics, but this was much more enjoyable.

The combat and weapon systems are good and a lot of fun. The story/dialogue is decent enough not to skip (I play for the violence and usually skip). My character (Carla) has an entertaining personality and attitude and is genuinely funny at times. The graphics are quite good as well. 

Some pet peeves (these aren't major problems, but just things I found annoying) -
'Puzzle' type quests with pressure valves or trying to find circuit breakers and stuff - usually not that hard, but repetitive and annoying. 

'Completing circuits with water - wut

Limited inventory - this was annoying because if forced you to do inventory management for no reason, as weapons you leave behind get sent to storage automatically anyway. So why not keep them on you? Limited inventory only makes sense if you want to limit the loot that can be sold. 

Also you could only hold a certain amount of ammo...I get that they wanted the focus to be melee weapons, but people should be able to play how they want. The limited ammo (and gunpowder) meant you couldn't really do a full quest without crafting/buying more ammo, and it wasn't always available.

Also, storage locker/workbench/vendor placement was kind of erratic. Why not have a storage locker whenever there's a workbench?
This game, along with the other Dead Island Games, needs Cross Platform Multiplayer. The fact they don't in this day and age is ridicules!

Ammo is a little scarce, so this is primarily a melee game. Love the way you can customize your weapons, enjoyed playing through all of the missions and side missions. A lot of content for your money if you get the ultimate edition on a deep sale (I got it for less than ten dollars). I found the combat somewhat challenging, but my reaction times are kinda slow now. If you're feeling the game is too easy, try equipping the masochist card. The card system has some flexibility to customize your playstyle, especially when you get to the autophage cards, which have bigger bonuses, but tradeoffs as well. This game is well worth the money if you like melee combat and get it on sale (deep sales happen fairly often now).

I have played the first Dead Island games back in 2011 and 2013 and loved the serie. I was so eager to play the sequel but when it entered development hell I was sure it would never release. But now that almost 13 years later we finally have Dead Island 2, I can finally say that this is a masterpiece. I love the game so much. It's so fun and addictive to the point that it makes me want to earn each and every achievement. It is refreshing to also see a game running on UE5 that is kinda optimised and works. 

It is a game that I recommend you buy with the DLC as well because it expands on the story and offers you awesome weapons to fight the undead with.

I thought this game would be trash considering all the problems it had while being developed, and the fact that I got it for under 5 dollars, but it ended up being a blast and I highly recommend it. I was able to beat the main story in under 20 hours solo. 

The last two missions are rather difficult if you choose to ignore side quests, like I did, but it's still manageable with enough attempts and adjusting strategies. 

It's not an open world game, but I wouldn't say it's a detriment to the gameplay or how fun it is. There are still plenty of areas to explore.

Played this game on Xbox Series X with all DLC's and enjoyed every second. Especially the little Easter eggs of the mission names referrencing past zombie movies to the animations of you killing zombies to the physics of the game. Plus the voice lines from all playable characters to non playable characters is top notch!

I wish I could've gotten into this game. I love First Person zombie games and zombie ♥♥♥♥ in general. Dying Light is one of my all timers, and I remember buying the OG Dead Island back on my 360 when it came out (bit disappointed there too lmao) and watching the first trailer for what was the OG Dead Island 2 before we got this. But as you can see from my playtime, this game has clearly just not clicked.

Maybe I'm just too used to what I'd consider a "good" first person zombie game w/ a focus on melee combat in the Dying Light franchise, but this just felt flaccid to me. My first character was Jacob, so I was mainly focused on light attacks-- except... light attacks felt bad. They were too slow, floaty, and lacked any sort of "oomph." Okay, my bad, I thought. If I wanted oomph, I should've played a heavy attack character. So Dani, right? Except... no. Heavy attacks ALSO felt really bad. First, idk why it locks you in place when you swing. That just doesn't feel good, especially since one of the best parts of combat in Dying Light, imo, is the "footsies" that you can play when it comes to lining up a perfect horizontal slap of a shovel and doming 4 zombies before they can grab onto you. Here? It's all sorts of janky feeling. Moreover, there seems to be some sort of soft reticle lock in place, likely due to how the limb damage is handled. While it LOOKS phenomenal (and a lot of what carried this game was that it LOOKED phenomenal), I'd take a hit to the gore system if it meant a better, more fluid melee system. Which, imho, this game does not have.

I'm glad for the others who loved the game, but it's just not for me, and whenever I redownload to scratch my zombie smashing itch, I quickly bounce off a little after I reach Emma Jaunt's house. Maybe I'm quitting too soon and I'm right on the precapice of when it "gets good". But for all the times I've started and restarted to make it work, this one just never hit.

The combat is the most fun I've had murdering zombies in a long time and the banter makes me giggle.

I've played a ton of zombie games, most of the protags are very very grim and serious. I like a little party at the end of the world.

Story ended... Awkwardly as frick tho, I am worried about how they're gonna manage to pick it up and not let it flop in Dead Island 3

The spawn rate to weapon durability ratio is dumb. Weapons don't last long enough to deal with endlessly spawning zombies. The game really punishes you for wanting to explore or if you take too long to find an objective. Zombies spawn out of thin air the second you turn around. While on the pier I had 8 screamers spawn one after the other with no breaks between. The weapons break fast and quickly fall behind in levels which makes investing in weapon upgrades mostly pointless except for bosses. Compared to the first game this one falls short on almost every level except for the graphics, but when the rest of the game is more frustrating than fun then what's the point?

TL;DR: It's Mafia 3 but with zombies.

The game itself is pretty enjoyable, it's a fun “brain-dead” shooter where you blast zombies. Good gore system, interesting mechanics BUT the situation repeats itself just like in Mafia 3. Just because the structure is good doesn’t mean the game delivers. The story is uninteresting and forcedly “being cool”, some mechanics, like the cards, become useless over time because they offer too little variety in gameplay. Plus, after exploring a location with regular zombies and heading to a new one where you’re just discovering a new enemy, it SUDDENLY turns out that enemy is in every previous location! This completely killed my desire to continue the adventure halfway through the game - I felt like I wasn’t making any progress.

im going to give it a 8/10
the story kept me playing the zeds however were annoying at times endlessly spawning even materializing right in front of me.

The NPC`s were fun to chat with some of them were annoying.

Didn`t like the lost and found quests was running around way to much to find what i was looking for, some moments were to predictable to even include in this review, and as always durability on certain weapons don`t make sense for example how does knuckle dusters have durability when its a solid peace of metal or a hammer when its made from durable metal and sole function is to bash objects.

I can understand bladed weapons may need to be maintained but weapons like a mace really com on! I could go on an on for ages about the lack of reality in zombie games.
firearms are understandable as moving parts may jam or break with use.

The idea of having different weapons for each zombie types is a step in the right direction but there is a line somewhere
also the different kinds of zeds make the game feel more alive like water carrier zeds. but you find that most of them you face are just the same in all zombie games one that`s strong (aka hard to kill), one that runs like hell, one that`s infectious leaving trails of slime or goop, one that just walks and blows up, and of course the spitter zombie. 

That`s my two cents
I would play it again with mods.

Dead Island 2 is a fun, chaotic zombie game that focuses more on action and gore-filled combat than serious horror. The game takes place in a zombie-infested Los Angeles, and the colorful locations make it stand out from darker zombie games. The environments are detailed, the weapons are creative, and fighting zombies feels satisfying because of the heavy melee combat and different ways to upgrade your gear.

One of the best parts of Dead Island 2 is the combat. Smashing through zombies with bats, blades, hammers, and modified weapons feels intense and entertaining. The game also gives you different characters to choose from, each with their own stats and playstyle, which makes the game more replayable. It is especially fun with friends because the co-op makes the zombie fights feel more chaotic and exciting.

However, the game can get repetitive after a while. A lot of the missions involve going from one area to another, fighting groups of zombies, and collecting items. The story is not the strongest part of the game, and some players might find the gameplay loop simple. Still, the game does a good job of keeping things entertaining through its combat, humor, and detailed zombie damage system.

Overall, Dead Island 2 is a great game if you enjoy zombie action, melee combat, weapon crafting, and co-op gameplay. It is not the deepest game, but it is fun, satisfying, and easy to enjoy when you just want to fight zombies and explore a destroyed version of Los Angeles.

Rating: 9/10

Great game - plenty of zombie whacking fun, great graphics, detailed and varied world, fun combat. There is a great mixture of melee and gun gameplay, and both are equally fun, which is a rarity. Recommended!

very corny/cliche story, and due to the HUGE visual upgrade compared to the first 2 games + the tense feeling of the MSQ (the side quests are NOT serious at all), the game feels like it takes itself way too seriously for what it is. it was fun to play with a friend and kick zombies off ledges, set the entire map on fire like Divinity Original Sin 2, and poke fun at the nonsense aspects of the story, like [spoiler]when you are talking to a bunch of people in a restaurant, and suddenly a huge zombie breaks the window, crushes some dudes head, and then... walks away? no other zombies, and the area remains a safe zone[/spoiler]. It was worth 7 dollars for sure, its a silly game just like the ones before it.

For anyone who wants to buy this game, here is a very simplified version of your gameplay (and i mean VERY simplified).

Here goes:....... "Locked, go find the key". (ALMOST everything wants a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ key, no joke.)

I did like it though. (Not the find the key part, most of the game is fine, it's just the endless amount of locked ♥♥♥♥ that needs keys. i could swear i found more locked things that needed a specific key than the amount of weapons, blueprints, materials AND healing items COMBINED.

i truly love this game. it can be challenging, but fun. i went a while without playing and every time i get back into it, it's still exciting lol. i haven't finished the game fully because i'm still working through SOLA, but i'm not even mad about that because i have something to look forward too every time i hop on this game :)
Bought this game a few days a go and I can say the game is pretty fun. Only thing that I disliked in this game was a side-story mission throwing 3 clotters at me.

The most seven outta ten game to ever do it.

Dead Island returns in mostly the same form in a new location. There's still random ass elemental effects in a mostly handwavy "it's got a battery so it does lightning damage" way, you're still mostly bludgeoning things. It's still a pretty okay game that falls frustratingly short of being excellent. Doesn't really do enough with the setting, there's some decent variety in that Beverly Hills is quite distinct from downtown and Santa Monica, but like... LA is a MASSIVE, diverse setting and it's kind of lame that there's a whole ass sewer section you go through at least twice when it could be any number of other neighbourhoods or even the big concrete maze of the LA river. 
The quest rewards are rarely interesting, the notes and sidecontent might as well not exist. The DLCs are inessential but at least distinct experiences. The plot goes some frankly insane places but you're not playing Dead Island for the story, we learned that the first time. But the minute to minute zombie bashing is still really fun, the way the gore and dismemberment works is satisfyingly meaty, the variety of enemies is mostly pretty good even if some are underused. Weapon variety FEELS pretty good, knuckles feel different than BIG HAMMER, which feels different than sword, as it should if you're gonna be pretty melee-centric. There's a decent spread of guns if you're a weirdo. They're there. It's an option. The card slot talent system sure is a "we had to have some progression" answer. It's not "plus .5% crit" bad and it's instantly swappable so it's at least not getting in the way too much, the whole autophage balancing act is... Entirely ignorable.

Putting aside the Epic-related ♥♥♥♥ because frankly who cares, it's a pretty okay action RPG for as long as it lasts. There's not a tremendous amount of post-game but does there really have to be? Sometimes things can just end, 25 hours or so is a decent amount of game. The non-sale price is WAY too steep but if you're buying a non-indie game three years after release at full price I don't know what can be done for you?

I feel like this game gets unfairly maligned. Get it on sale and grab a buddy or two and this game is a great time. The melee is weighty and satisfying, the story and characters (or at least Jacob) are kinda funny, and the scenery is gorgeous. It took every lesson from Dead Island 1 and Dying Light and actually LEARNED from them.

 Genuinely one of my best purchases of 2026.
corny, fun little game for mindless fun

Visually the game is appealing, the game's dialogue and humor is something I can only describe "campy millennial vibes," so anyone born over 1998 may get tired of it real fast. Aside from that, the variety of characters are decent, having enough personality and style to at least have them stand out from each other.
After a few playthroughs, you'll realize the gameplay is extremely repetitive, but it works for a session of mindless self-indulgence.

Would not get this game unless it's on sale. I got this for like $10-15, would not shill out a full $50 for it.

Before reading this, do note that I completed the game on Ryan on the Epic version, I bought this game on Steam so I could have the best edition and all the fun things Steam gives me.

That being said, I loved my time with Dead Island 2. The graphics are phenomenal, and the gameplay is very satisfying. The game changes up the series usual levelling systems by introducing cards, which you can change at any point unlike Dead Island and Riptide's system that prevented you from doing this.

The story is alright, not too great but it's far from the worst. Not gonna talk much about it as honestly it was quite forgettable, but it wasn't so bad that it made me want to stop playing. 

There is going to be a sequel, just hoping it doesn't take another decade to come out after the trailer like this one did, but considering this game went through like 3 reworks before landing on this one, I doubt it'll take that long, especially considering the general praise this game has gotten with the current devs.



PS: Kinda weird this is Dead Island 2 considering Riptide is a sequel of DI1, which should technically make it DI2 no?

The game is very good, aside from SOLA which had I bought it separately I would want a refund. Absolute crap that makes no sense and long winded speeches I skipped because they made no sense. If anyone claims to have made a plot for it, FIRE THEM. I know this game has had a resurgence on console, do NOT release another SOLA. A great game tainted by no coherent writing or point to SOLA. Get the rest skip that.

Really wanted to like this game, trying over and over, this game just won't play, and when it does, it's after trying to get it to load like 30 times, using every method to get it to work, closing out background apps etc, verifying file integrity, uninstalled and reinstalled... nothing, have 103 mins of gameplay, only around 50 or so is actually playing, the rest is just trying to get it to start up and not crash during start up. when it did play graphics looked good, though I hated every single playable character, nothing like the original and riptide, at least they had my favorite character Sam B make an appearance in it, except the game crashed when i spoke to him and haven't been able to get it to play since. I would request a full refund if I didn't get this as a bundle with Dead Island 1 and Dead Island Riptide. At least those ones play with no issues. Combat wise, not a fan, Weapons break so quickly early game it's ridiculous, literally have to swing the weapons like crazy just to kill one zombie and in that time you're surrounded by other zombies and get killed. I like the NPC characters more than the actual main playable characters, would have loved to see a create a character option this time around with how long it took Dead Island 2 to be made after Dead Island Riptide. I will update my review on this game IF and Once I get the game to actually function and stop crashing and get further into the gameplay. Kinda wishing I would have bought it on Console Since I have Dead Island and Dead Island Riptide on Console, but since I saw the bundle on Steam with all three games I couldn't resist..
If you want it to be more challenging, I recommend rushing through the story quests so you can play NG+. Tho NG+ still feels a bit easy, but it’s aight. Story’s good.
This game is so much fun and has such a good pace, i can run and hide from zombies in a giant mansion, or sit on a roof and drop pipebombs and enjoy the beautiful sunset
very optimized game, it also looks real as hell. very fun gameplay too and very detailed accurate environment design
gameplay is fun characters are fun but quests are repetitive

Dead Island 2 is the most fun I've had playing any form of zombie game. The story is a good length while also being entertaining. I will say the rarest achievement is the most bs with extremely overpowered enemies. But other than that the game is balanced as it allows you to level yourself and your weapons as the game progresses in difficulty. Love the creativity, easter eggs, and pop culture references.

Would give this one a mixed/thumbs sideways if I could. The game has been in development hell for a long time, so the fact it's here and playable is a miracle. The combat is visceral and reactive, and fighting zombies is always enjoyable. The build system isn't quite as in depth as the earlier games but it's enough

My big complaints come from the repetitiveness, terrible plot (albeit somewhat to be expected after Riptide), and the design of the quests/open world. A lot of the core gameplay loop is killing zombies -> flip a switch -> wander around an area to kill a big guy -> find chest. Plenty of that can be obtuse at times due to the lack of information combined with how painful some of the areas are to explore.

If you can get past the sloggy parts and tolerate the core gameplay loop, it's a fun time. Better with friends I'm sure
Def my favorite game ngl i played dying light on epic games and this games literally the same but i like the gore better in this one. First Dead Island is just as great too!
